title: Week of April 6, 2026
summary: This week focused on making OpsPal more reliable and easier to use. The platform now automatically fixes common setup issues like missing dependencies and outdated configurations, while new data quality checks ensure your integrations stay compliant with business policies. Several HubSpot performance improvements bring it up to par with Salesforce capabilities.

entries.headline: HubSpot integration now matches Salesforce performance capabilities
Added automated compliance checks for data quality policies
details: Platform now verifies all data operations meet your business compliance requirements
Advisory-only checks provide recommendations without blocking workflows
Faster processing when dealing with unrelated commands

entries.headline: Platform updates now include automatic cleanup and maintenance
details: Removes old temporary files older than 7 days
Clears stuck session files after 5 minutes
Alerts you if memory usage files get too large

entries.headline: Platform now removes broken integrations automatically
details: Works across all operating systems including Windows
Detects and removes any outdated marketplace integrations
Applies to all installed plugins, not just specific ones

entries.headline: Resolved three critical Salesforce integration issues
details: Dynamic Forms now work properly when adding new fields to page layouts
New record types automatically get proper administrator permissions
Workflow routing no longer blocks sessions with keyword mismatches

entries.headline: Platform automatically installs missing components at startup
details: Detects and fixes missing dependencies when you start a session
Shows clear error messages and fix instructions when auto-repair fails
Runs repairs in background without interrupting your work
